locked.master_promise_source: Value Props v2 Part 2 — say it once, everywhere

copy.hero_title: One neutral engine. The disputes of a defined population.
copy.lead: Families, workplaces, community associations, campuses, and the people a public body serves — each with its own disputes, all served by the same identical, citation-grounded comparison.
copy.substance: What changes across markets is the population and the dispute type. What never changes is the process: symmetric intake, the same analysis run for each side to the same depth, a citation-grounded comparison, and a verifiable record. Families is live today on NY divorce and custody; the other markets are staged behind their own readiness and counsel gates. Every market inherits the same rule — the people, with the professionals they choose, decide.
copy.tier2_frame: One neutral engine, applied to the disputes of a defined population.
copy.master_promise: Both sides reach the table on the same honest picture, so the conversation starts where judgment matters. Truth · Fairness · Efficiency.
proof_points: The same seven stages run identically for every party, in every market — the process does not bend to the population.
Families is live on NY divorce and custody; Businesses, Communities & HOAs, Campuses, and Government are staged behind readiness and counsel gates.
Organization markets carry a Prevent → Resolve → Improve lifecycle, not just a dispute event.
coverage_tags: Roadmap: Businesses, Communities & HOAs, Campuses, Government

example_matter.body: The Families market, worked end to end. Two parties, one identical process — each side summarized to the same depth, then convergence and divergence laid side by side, objectively and with citations. The same engine runs a workplace or HOA dispute; only the population and the matter type change.

disclosure_block.standing_line: AI-assisted overviews · figures illustrative · not legal advice.
disclosure_block.keeps_this_honest: JusticeX is software for the objective comparison of legal disputes — not a law firm, lawyer, mediator, or arbitrator. It does not give legal advice, interpret the law, recommend a settlement, or predict an outcome. The people, with the professionals they choose, decide; court access is preserved.
disclosure_block.coverage_statement: Live today: two-party objective comparison for NY divorce & custody, in the Families market. Businesses, Communities & HOAs, Campuses & schools, and Government are roadmap and are not offered today.

gemini_notebook_brief.subject: How one neutral engine serves the disputes of five different populations without changing the process — and what is live today versus roadmap.
source_docs: sales-materials-overview:the 4 market 1-pagers — Families · Business · Communities & HOAs · Campuses & Schools (Appendix H, 20260628) — the ONLY authored source for the Communities and Campuses markets
key_points: One engine, five populations: Families, Businesses, Communities & HOAs, Campuses, Government.
The population and dispute type change; the symmetric, citation-grounded process does not.
Families/NY divorce & custody is live; the rest are staged behind readiness and counsel gates.
gemini_notebook_brief.studio_defaults: Audio=Brief/Shorter · Video=Brief ~60–90s/Classic+custom palette · Infographic=orientation+Standard · Deck=Presenter ~8 slides PDF+PPTX
gemini_notebook_brief.disclosure_line: AI-assisted overviews · figures illustrative · not legal advice.

higgsfield_brief.art_direction: Documentary, monochrome-leaning, calm; hexagon motif; five distinct empty rooms — a kitchen table, a workplace meeting room, a community hall, a campus office, a civic waiting area — rendered abstractly in one consistent light. No faces, no gavel. No flags or institutional insignia.
higgsfield_brief.negative_constraints: no fabricated human faces; no scales/gavel/courtroom; no stock handshakes; no off-palette

avatar.disclosure: AI-assisted presenter — shown at first exposure (EU AI Act Art. 50).
